title: Enduro School Mission Statement: EVERYBODY is a pro enduro athlete now, not just the fastest few

enrichment.summary: Kyle introduces 'Enduro School,' his personal mission to democratize professional athletic status by combining endurance training (triathlon) with daily audiobook listening and video commentary. He argues the current system only allows a tiny elite to be full-time athletes, and proposes that everyone can become a 'pro' by training all day while consuming books and contributing educational content to YouTube, funded by wealthy patrons or a UBI-style system.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Only the richest and most talented can currently be pros; the system should be leveled so everyone trains full-time
Toxic and fake, especially Instagram; people only show curated, dishonest versions of themselves
Rich people parasitically drain society's resources and should fund 'enduro schoolers' instead
Dominated by shallow wealth-flexing content that is like 'junk food' and should be crowded out by book-review videos
Fake and pushes people toward racist and politically motivated views
Got rich by cheating people; is intellectually unfit and would fail at enduro school
The $12,000/year basic income is roughly the right model to fund this lifestyle
Getting ripped just to show off is a waste of time when the world has bigger problems; mind and body should improve together
Doing imperfect daily videos is better than perfectionism; sharing ideas sparked by a book has real value even without perfect recall

entities: Dan Payne ("$50 billion man", motivational speaker)
